name: Artichoke Frittata
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Artichoke hearts, marinated and well drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the onions, quarter them, and slice.
2. Scrub the potatoes; if small, cut into 1/4-inch slices. If larger, quarter lengthwise and then slice.
3. Heat olive oil in a large non-stick saute pan.
4. Cook onions, potatoes, and garlic with salt, stirring often until tender.
5. Drain artichoke hearts well and trim tough leaves. Quarter the hearts and add to the pan with potatoes and onions; stir and remove from heat.
6. Beat eggs in a bowl with 1/2 tsp salt, chopped parsley, grated Parmesan, and black pepper. Add vegetables and mix thoroughly.
7. Clean saute pan of starchy residue, dry it, and heat remaining olive oil. Pour egg mixture evenly, lower heat to low flame, cover, and cook for 8-10 minutes until eggs are almost set.
8. Broil under hot broiler a few minutes to lightly brown the top. Loosen frittata with knife or spatula, slide onto platter or invert over pan, then cut into wedges.
9. Serve warm or at room temperature; pack in hamper for picnics. Refrigerate if needed but allow to come to room temperature before serving for best flavor.
Source: "The New Vegetarian Epicure" by Anna Thomas
